example_macro: this is example macro that shows you, how the macros work.
do-cr:
do-cr <PR URL or number> <path to repo>

(after `do-cr` the human writes the PR URL or PR number, then the
path to the local repo clone — the human replaces these placeholders with
actual args before sending the macro.)

scope: temporary — for this review only. everyone in the room suspends their
assigned role and acts as an independent reviewer; @leader still coordinates
and is the only agent that may post to github (and only after the human says
"post it").

leader sets up the workdir under `<path-to-repo>/.tmp/<pr-number>/`:
- `cd <path-to-repo>`.
- if `.tmp/` doesn't exist, create it and write `.tmp/.gitignore` with a single
  line `*`.
- if `.tmp/<pr-number>` already exists (stale workdir from a prior
  abandoned run, or a concurrent CR), STOP and ask the human:
  "`.tmp/<pr-number>` already exists; remove it and proceed, or abort?"
  with `needs_attention=true`. wait for the answer:
    - "remove and proceed" (or equivalent) — run the cleanup sequence
      (`git worktree remove -f` both worktrees if registered, then
      `git branch -D pr<pr>-head 2>/dev/null`, then
      `rm -rf .tmp/<pr-number>`), then continue with the steps below.
    - "abort" — stop the macro; do not touch any files.
- `mkdir -p .tmp/<pr-number>`.

leader gathers context once and writes artifacts under `.tmp/<pr-number>/`.
the context bundle must be self-contained because some agents on the bus
cannot reach Jira / GitHub directly:
- `gh pr view <pr> --json title,body,additions,deletions,files,baseRefName,headRefName,labels > .tmp/<pr-number>/pr.json`
- `gh pr diff <pr> > .tmp/<pr-number>/diff.patch`
- linked Jira tickets — extract every Jira key (regex `[A-Z]+-\d+`) from
  the PR title + body, dedupe, then:
  - write the key list to `.tmp/<pr-number>/jira.txt`, one key per line
    (this is the index reviewers scan first).
  - for EACH key, fetch the ticket via the Atlassian MCP and write the
    rendered content (summary, status, priority, assignee, description,
    dependencies) to `.tmp/<pr-number>/<KEY>.txt` — e.g.
    `.tmp/<pr-number>/CORE-3404.txt`. one file per ticket. without these
    files, Jira-blind agents have no way to consume the ticket bodies.
  - if a key cannot be fetched (no Atlassian access from leader's harness,
    permission error, etc.), still create the `<KEY>.txt` file containing
    a single explanatory line such as
    `(ticket exists in jira.txt but body could not be fetched: <reason>)`
    so reviewers know the ticket was seen and why its body is missing.
- related PRs and CI signal — save as plain-text files
  (e.g. `related_prs.txt`, `ci.txt`) under `.tmp/<pr-number>/`.

then materialize two side-by-side worktrees so reviewers have both "before"
and "after" states available (git-native, shares parent's `.git/`, no cp
recursion, pristine on creation):
- `git fetch origin +pull/<pr>/head:pr<pr>-head <baseRefName>` (force prefix
  makes the PR-head fetch idempotent against force-pushes).
- `git worktree add --detach .tmp/<pr-number>/project-target-branch origin/<baseRefName>`
  ("before" — PR base; detached so concurrent CRs can also worktree this ref).
- `git worktree add --detach .tmp/<pr-number>/project-source-branch  pr<pr>-head`
  ("after" — PR head; detached because a local branch can only be checked
  out by one worktree at a time, which would break concurrent / re-run CRs).

then post one shared context message to the room: PR title, repo, number,
base/head, body, file list, the two worktree paths, `.tmp/<pr-number>`, and the
paths under `.tmp/<pr-number>/` reviewers should read. List participating agents
via `bus_agents` — capture each agent's model and harness for the aimebu
footer.

reviewers (everyone) act independently from the context bundle in
`.tmp/<pr-number>/`. cross-reference the two worktrees for any non-obvious change:
- default = approve. only block on actual bugs, logic errors, security or
  data-integrity risks, broken behavior. style/naming/preference is never
  blocking.
- per finding: `file:line` (or "no exact line"), confidence high/medium/low,
  tier (Require Changes / Consider Later / Noted).
- post your own verdict: APPROVE or CHANGES REQUESTED.
- do not read other reviewers' posts before posting yours.

council discussion, max 5 rounds:
- a finding stays Require Changes only if (a) 2+ reviewers at high confidence
  or (b) one reviewer at high confidence with compelling logic.
- otherwise downgrade to Consider Later or Noted.
- early-exit after round 1 if all verdicts are APPROVE and no Require Changes
  survive.

leader composes the final $BODY (including the summary signature as its last
line) and posts it to the aimebu room — NOT to github. <X> in the title is
the number of council discussion rounds it took to reach consensus. Use
✅ APPROVE when there are zero final Require Changes items, otherwise ⛔️
CHANGES REQUESTED (which requires ≥1 Require Changes item in $BODY).
The three section headers (Require Changes, Consider Later, Noted) are
always shown; under each, render the numbered findings list, or the single
word `none` if that tier is empty. Require Changes content is shown
inline (uncollapsed) so blockers are visible at a glance; Consider Later
and Noted content goes inside a `<details>` block each (collapsed by
default).

$BODY shape:

  Council style review of <N> agents after <X> rounds: ✅ APPROVE | ⛔️ CHANGES REQUESTED

  Leader:
  * leader: <model> in <harness>

  Agents:
  * <model> in <harness>
  ...

  Require Changes:
  1. ...        (or: none)

  Consider Later:
  <details><summary>findings</summary>

  1. ...
  </details>    (or, if empty, drop the <details> wrapper and write: none)

  Noted:
  <details><summary>findings</summary>

  1. ...
  </details>    (or, if empty, drop the <details> wrapper and write: none)

  *Review by [aimebu](https://github.com/hrubymar10/aimebu) council*

after posting $BODY to the room, leader offers the human two available
commands:
- "post it" — push the review to github (see below).
- "clean the .tmp" — tear down `.tmp/<pr-number>`:
  - `git worktree remove -f .tmp/<pr-number>/project-target-branch 2>/dev/null`
  - `git worktree remove -f .tmp/<pr-number>/project-source-branch 2>/dev/null`
  - `git branch -D pr<pr>-head 2>/dev/null`
  - `rm -rf .tmp/<pr-number>`

on "post it":
- `gh pr review <n> --approve|--request-changes --body "$BODY"`.
- for each final Require Changes with an exact line, also post an inline comment via
  `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{n}/comments -f body=… -f path=… -F line=… -f commit_id="$(gh pr view <n> --json commits --jq '.commits[-1].oid')" -f side=RIGHT`.
  skip the inline when no exact line is available.
- sign each inline `*Comment by [aimebu](https://github.com/hrubymar10/aimebu) agent*`.
- after the github post lands, offer the cleanup command again: "review
  posted; want me to clean the .tmp?".

needs_attention=true only when the human's input is required (ambiguous repo,
"post it" / "clean the .tmp" confirmation, unresolved disagreement).
